pokemon:omanyte.name: Omanyte
pokemon:omanyte.summary: Omanyte is one of the ancient and long-since-extinct Pokémon that have been regenerated from fossils by people. If attacked by an enemy, it withdraws itself inside its hard shell.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Omanyte is one of the ancient and long-since-extinct Pokémon that have been regenerated from fossils by people. If attacked by an enemy, it withdraws itself inside its hard shell.
A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.
A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.
Although long extinct, in rare cases, it can be genetically resurrected from fossils.
In prehistoric times, it swam on the sea floor, eating plankton. Its fossils are sometimes found.
A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.
One of the ancient and long-since-extinct POKéMON that have been regenerated from fossils by humans. If attacked, it withdraws into its hard shell.
A prehistoric POKéMON that lived in the primordial sea, it swims by twisting its 10 tentacles about.
Revived from an ancient fossil, this POKéMON uses air stored in its shell to sink and rise in water.
Revived from an ancient fossil, this Pokémon uses air stored in its shell to sink and rise in water.
Although long extinct, in rare cases, it can be genetically regenerated from fossils.
An ancient Pokémon that was recovered from a fossil. It swam by cleverly twisting its 10 tentacles about.
An ancient Pokémon that was recovered from a fossil. It swam by cleverly twisting its 10 tentacles about.
Omanyte is one of the ancient and long-since-extinct Pokémon that have been regenerated from fossils by people. If attacked by an enemy, it withdraws itself inside its hard shell.
A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.
A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.
Although long extinct, in rare cases, it can be genetically resurrected from fossils.
OMANYTE is one of the ancient and long- since-extinct POKéMON that have been regenerated from fossils by people. If attacked by an enemy, it withdraws itself inside its hard shell.
OMANYTE is one of the ancient and long- since-extinct POKéMON that have been regenerated from fossils by people. If attacked by an enemy, it withdraws itself inside its hard shell.
This Pokémon is a member of an ancient, extinct species. Omanyte paddles through water with its 10 tentacles, looking like it’s just drifting along.
This POKéMON from ancient times is said to have navi­ gated the sea by adeptly twisting its 10 tentacles.
This Pokémon from ancient times is said to have navigated the sea by adeptly twisting its 10 tentacles.
Because some Omanyte manage to escape after being restored or are released into the wild by people, this species is becoming a problem.
It was restored from an ancient fossil. Those Helix Fossils are excavated from areas that were once oceans long, long ago.
Omanyte lived in the seas of antiquity. Its fossils have been found bearing bite marks from Archeops, so apparently Archeops preyed on it.
A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.
A Pokémon that was resurrected from a fossil using modern science. It swam in ancient seas.
A prehistoric Pokémon that lived in the primordial sea, it swims by twisting its 10 tentacles about.
Revived from an ancient fossil, this Pokémon uses air stored in its shell to sink and rise in water.
An ancient POKéMON that was recovered from a fossil. It swims by cleverly twisting its 10 tentacles about.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Omanyte is one of the ancient and long-since-extinct Pokémon that have been regenerated from fossils by people. If attacked by an enemy, it withdraws itself inside its hard shell.

pokemon:omastar.name: Omastar
pokemon:omastar.summary: Omastar uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large and heavy, causing its movements to become too slow and ponderous.
expansionNotes: Competitive, encounter, and media coverage can be expanded later without changing the page contract.

pokedexEntries.text: Omastar uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large and heavy, causing its movements to become too slow and ponderous.
It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.
It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.
A prehistoric POKéMON that died out when its heavy shell made it impossible to catch prey.
Its heavy shell allowed it to reach only nearby food. This could be the reason it is extinct.
It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.
An OMASTAR uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large, making its movements slow and ponderous.
Its tentacles are highly developed as if they are hands and feet. As soon as it ensnares prey, it bites.
Apparently, it cracked SHELLDER's shell with its sharp fangs and sucked out the insides.
Apparently, it cracked SHELLDER’s shell with its sharp fangs and sucked out the insides.
Despite having strong fangs and tentacles, it went extinct when its heavy shell made it unable to catch prey.
Its sharp beak rings its mouth. Its shell was too big for it to move freely, so it became extinct.
Its sharp beak rings its mouth. Its shell was too big for it to move freely, so it became extinct.
Omastar uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large and heavy, causing its movements to become too slow and ponderous.
It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.
It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.
A prehistoric POKéMON that died out when its heavy shell made it impossible to catch prey.
OMASTAR uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large and heavy, causing its movements to become too slow and ponderous.
OMASTAR uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large and heavy, causing its movements to become too slow and ponderous.
Omastar’s sharp fangs could crush rock, but the Pokémon can attack only the prey that come within reach of its tentacles.
Once wrapped around its prey, it never lets go. It eats the prey by tearing at it with sharp fangs.
Once wrapped around its prey, it never lets go. It eats the prey by tearing at it with sharp fangs.
Weighed down by a large and heavy shell, Omastar couldn’t move very fast. Some say it went extinct because it was unable to catch food.
It wraps its prey in its tentacles to immobilize them and then finishes them off with its sharp fangs.
Its heavy shell is thought to be the reason this ancient Pokémon died out. It’s apparently a distant ancestor of Octillery.
It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.
It is thought that this Pokémon became extinct because its spiral shell grew too large.
Its tentacles are highly developed as if they are hands and feet. As soon as it ensnares prey, it bites.
Once wrapped around its prey, it never lets go. It eats the prey by tearing at it with sharp fangs.
Sharp beaks ring its mouth. Its shell was too big for it to move freely, so it became extinct.

competitiveSummary: Competitive notes are pending a richer source import.
loreSummary: Omastar uses its tentacles to capture its prey. It is believed to have become extinct because its shell grew too large and heavy, causing its movements to become too slow and ponderous.
